_meta.hypothesis: Advanced players have distinct shot sequence signatures that could power a coaching product

discoveries.title: The '4.0 Fingerprint': speed_up→block→speed_up Chain Exclusive to 4.0+ Players
discoveries.finding: The attack-defend-attack chain (speed_up→block→speed_up) appears exclusively in 4.0+ DUPR clips and is absent from all lower bands. Distribution: 4.0-4.5 = 7 instances, 3.5-4.0 = 0, 3.0-3.5 = 0.
discoveries.why_it_matters: Concrete 3-shot sequence to grade players on — directly validates coaching product hypothesis.
discoveries.sampling_bias_flag: Only 4 clips in 4.0+ band — may reflect 1-2 specific recurring players at venue.
discoveries.strongest_counter_argument: With only 4 clips in the 4.0+ band, pattern could be person-specific not skill-level-specific.

discoveries.title: Advanced Players Use Dinks as Setup Weapons — Measurably Higher Attack Conversion Rate
discoveries.finding: 3.5+ DUPR players convert dinks to speed-up attacks at a higher rate than 3.0-3.5 players. The behavioral signature: dink strategically to create an opening, then attack. Beginners dink to survive.
discoveries.why_it_matters: Teachable metric: dink attack conversion rate measurable before/after coaching intervention.
discoveries.sampling_bias_flag: Highlights over-represent exciting rallies — may inflate visible attack rates.
discoveries.strongest_counter_argument: Dink-to-speed-up rate may reflect highlight clip type (exciting rallies selected), not true skill difference.

discoveries.title: Speed-Up Is the Riskiest Shot: 12.5% Win Rate, 20.8% Error Rate
discoveries.finding: 24 tracked speed-ups: 3 winners (12.5%), 5 errors (20.8%), 16 rally continuations (66.7%). The 'be aggressive at the kitchen' coaching trope is not data-supported in this corpus.
discoveries.why_it_matters: Shot selection coach alert — AI flags statistically suboptimal speed-up timing in real time.
discoveries.sampling_bias_flag: Highlights over-represent exciting rallies — speed-up outcomes may be skewed in both directions.
discoveries.strongest_counter_argument: Highlight selection captures more long rallies where speed-ups appear mid-rally, not the clean winners from short aggressive points.

discoveries.title: Kitchen Mastery Is the Best Single DUPR Predictor — Linear 3.0→8.0 Ladder
discoveries.finding: Kitchen mastery rating climbs monotonically across DUPR bands: 2.5-3.0=3.0, 3.0-3.5=5.9, 3.5-4.0=7.1, 4.0-4.5=8.0. More linear than court IQ, touch, or power.
discoveries.why_it_matters: North star coaching metric — 'Raise your Kitchen Score' — simple and measurable.
discoveries.sampling_bias_flag: AI-estimated DUPR from same model doing analysis creates circular reasoning risk.
discoveries.strongest_counter_argument: Gemini may correlate kitchen proximity with skill in both DUPR estimation and mastery rating — artificial correlation.

discoveries.title: Advanced Players Close Rallies ~40% Faster Than Intermediate Players
discoveries.finding: Avg rally length by DUPR: 2.5-3.0=2.0, 3.0-3.5=13.6, 3.5-4.0=10.6, 4.0-4.5=8.2 shots. 4.0+ players are ~40% more decisive than 3.5 players.
confidence_caps_applied: CRITICAL highlight bias on rally length
discoveries.why_it_matters: Decisiveness quotient — trackable coaching metric for shot selection efficiency.
discoveries.sampling_bias_flag: CRITICAL: Long rallies preferentially highlighted — beginner rally lengths are almost certainly inflated.
discoveries.strongest_counter_argument: This finding likely does not survive cross with full match data — entirely explainable by highlight selection algorithm behavior.
